ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sensor_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor data entry */,
  "time" REAL /* Timestamp of the data collection */,
  "bot_react_x" REAL /* Reaction force in the x-direction from the robot's bot */,
  "bot_react_y" REAL /* Reaction force in the y-direction from the robot's bot */,
  "disp_x639" REAL /* Displacement in the x-direction for sensor 639 */,
  "disp_y639" REAL /* Displacement in the y-direction for sensor 639 */,
  "top_react_x" REAL /* Reaction force in the x-direction from the robot's top */,
  "top_react_y" REAL /* Reaction force in the y-direction from the robot's top */,
  "robot_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the robot collecting the data */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the sensor used to collect the data */,
  "operational_condition_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the operational condition during data collection */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the location where the data was collected */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the data collection process */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_data_robot_id FOREIGN KEY ("robot_id") REFERENCES robots ("robot_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_data_operational_condition_id FOREIGN KEY ("operational_condition_id") REFERENCES operational_conditions ("condition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_data_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_data_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensor_types ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "robots" (
  "robot_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each robot */,
  "robot_name" TEXT /* Name assigned to the robot */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the robot */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department responsible for the robot */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries related to the robot */,
  "manufacturer_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the robot's manufacturer */,
  "maintenance_interval" INTEGER /* Interval (in days) for scheduled maintenance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("robot_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_robots_manufacturer_id FOREIGN KEY ("manufacturer_id") REFERENCES manufacturers ("manufacturer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "operational_conditions" (
  "condition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each operational condition */,
  "condition_name" TEXT /* Name of the operational condition */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the operational condition */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the operational condition */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("condition_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_analysis" (
  "analysis_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data analysis entry */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the original sensor data */,
  "condition_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operational condition during the data collection */,
  "average_bot_react_x" REAL /* Average reaction force in the x-direction from the robot's bot over the data set */,
  "average_bot_react_y" REAL /* Average reaction force in the y-direction from the robot's bot over the data set */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the analysis was performed */,
  "analysis_result" TEXT /* Summary of the analysis results */,
  "recommendations" TEXT /* Recommendations based on the analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analysis_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_analysis_condition_id FOREIGN KEY ("condition_id") REFERENCES operational_conditions ("condition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_analysis_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES sensor_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, data analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor data being accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "session_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access session in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES sensor_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric entry */,
  "robot_id" INTEGER /* ID of the robot for which the metric is recorded */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the performance metric */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the metric was recorded */,
  "benchmark" REAL /* Benchmark value for comparison */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_robot_id FOREIGN KEY ("robot_id") REFERENCES robots ("robot_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_types" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor type */,
  "sensor_name" TEXT /* Name of the sensor */,
  "sensor_type" TEXT /* Type of sensor (e.g., displacement, reaction force) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sensor and its purpose */,
  "accuracy_specification" TEXT /* Accuracy specification of the sensor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_quality" (
  "quality_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each quality assessment entry */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor data being assessed */,
  "quality_score" REAL /* Quality score assigned to the data (on a scale of 0 to 100) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the quality assessment was performed */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments regarding the quality assessment */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating of the data quality (1-5) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("quality_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_quality_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES sensor_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "robot_maintenance" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance record */,
  "robot_id" INTEGER /* ID of the robot that underwent maintenance */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date when the maintenance was performed */,
  "details" TEXT /* Details of the maintenance performed */,
  "next_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Scheduled date for the next maintenance */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance performed (e.g., routine, corrective) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_robot_maintenance_robot_id FOREIGN KEY ("robot_id") REFERENCES robots ("robot_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the location */,
  "environment_type" TEXT /* Type of environment (e.g., indoor, outdoor, controlled) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "manufacturers" (
  "manufacturer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each manufacturer */,
  "manufacturer_name" TEXT /* Name of the manufacturer */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the manufacturer */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the manufacturer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("manufacturer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "collaborations" (
  "collaboration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each collaboration entry */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project being collaborated on */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user participating in the collaboration */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user in the collaboration (e.g., lead, contributor) */,
  "join_date" TEXT /* Date when the user joined the collaboration */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("collaboration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_collaborations_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_collaborations_project_id FOREIGN KEY ("project_id") REFERENCES projects ("project_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "projects" (
  "project_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each project */,
  "project_name" TEXT /* Name of the project */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the project */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the project */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the project */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the project (e.g., in progress, completed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("project_id")
)
